upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal strictly widens the parent one-sided first-stage model by allowing bounded first-stage defier mass and includes the required kappa=0 parent reduction.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dynamic_iv_compliance/v2: one-sided first-stage memory polytope; this adds bounded-defier nesting and the zero-defier corner

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997: finite response-type sharp IV bounds; generic feasible-set nesting is routine

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dynamic_iv_compliance/v2: pure never-taker response-contribution subtraction; pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance: static bounded-defier envelope

published_prior_art.cite: Noack2021: defier-share monotonicity sensitivity bounds; DahlHuberMellace2023: LATE with/without defiers; neither has dynamic retained-memory branches

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dynamic_iv_compliance/v2 has strict memory gain only at q_F=0; pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance has a static point-identification phase statement

published_prior_art.cite: BugniGaoObradovicVelez2024 and DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 cover static CAR/generic discrete bounds, not a defier-budget memory-gain threshold

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist misses Noack2021, a closer defier-share monotonicity-sensitivity paper deriving sharp bounds indexed by defier share; the proposal should explain why retained dynamic assignment memory is not just the dynamic analogue of that sensitivity analysis.

novelty_flags.label: proposal-level publishability floor
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; to reach flagship, the kernel needs a more general multi-period or generic-class threshold result rather than a narrow two-stage binary analytic LP/prism upgrade.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The feasible defier-share interval omits the always-taker nonnegativity constraint q_A(f)=Pr(D_1=1|Z_1=0,X=x)-f>=0, so the displayed union can include impossible first-stage strata.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the missing q_A(f)>=0 constraint to F_x(P,kappa) and propagate it through the prism and proof sketch.

Add explicit comparisons to Noack2021 and deChaisemartin2017, isolating why bounded-defier retained-memory sharp bounds are not covered by static monotonicity-violation sensitivity or compliers-defiers assumptions.

To meet the flagship floor, strengthen Conjecture 2 from a one-cell two-stage threshold into a generic-class theorem over multi-period retained-memory systems or an open-set characterization with a closed-form threshold.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 1's feasible-share definition, thicken the defier-sensitivity literature comparison, and decide whether to broaden the threshold theorem enough to justify flagship targeting.
